sections.title: Using Letters in Venn Problems
points: Sometimes a region is labelled with a letter like x or y.
We write an equation to find that unknown value.
Read the question carefully to know which regions are included.

sections.title: Whole Circle Equations
points: If n(A) is given, it includes both A only and the intersection.
Example: x + 10 = 25 means the whole circle A has 25 members.
Solve the equation to find x.

sections.title: Total Class Equation
points: When total pupils are given, add every region including outside.
Solve the sum to find the unknown value.

sections.title: Intersection Trick
points: If n(A) and n(B) are both given, the overlap may be counted twice.

sections.text: Always substitute the value back into the question before answering. If x = 15 and the question asks for 2x, answer 30.

cards.fact: A letter representing an unknown number in a set region.
cards.mnemonic: Solve the unknown like a mystery.

cards.fact: The total of a set including the intersection.
cards.mnemonic: Whole circle = all pieces in that circle.

cards.fact: The sum of all regions, including outside the circles.
Use (A + B) - Total = Intersection when A and B overlap.
cards.mnemonic: Double count minus total gives the overlap.
